The AI industry is transitioning from chasing the smartest frontier models to optimizing intelligence per dollar, as inference costs plummet and enterprises shift from pilots to production-scale economics, causing most routine workloads to favor efficient models over premium ones

Prediction Markets.text:
The World as Model - on sensing, representations, and the financialization of the physical world
Just as high-frequency trading firms won by building high-fidelity representations of market data, a new generation of companies is capturing physical infrastructure at its native resolution to create a digital representation layer
Mapping reality through physics-informed models instead of linguistic bottlenecks allows these platforms to accurately predict asset behavior and reshape how trillions of dollars in industrial assets are financed, insured, and traded
https://cruciblecapital.substack.com/p/the-world-as-model

tweet.text:
The World Cup is attracting lots of interest and activity, and other areas are growing alongside. Non-sports volume — across categories like politics, economics, geopolitics, and current events — hit $3.6B last week on Kalshi and Polymarket combined. That’s larger than total prediction market volume (sports included) was just last year.

In July 2025, non-sports weekly volume was around $200M. It has grown roughly 18x since then, with the trajectory steepening sharply this month.

Indonesia jails Gojek (one of Southeast Asia's largest ride-hailing app) founder Nadiem for 10 years, orders US$48 million restitution
Nadiem was charged with corruption for allegedly abusing his authority to enrich himself through the rigged/improper procurement of Google Chromebooks for schools during 2020-2022
Prosecutors say he manipulated the process (switching from Windows despite known rural internet problems), caused ~$125m+ in state losses, and had a conflict of interest tied to Google’s investment in Gojek
https://www.businesstimes.com.sg/international/asean/indonesia-jails-gojek-founder-nadiem-10-years-orders-us48-million-restitution

BlackBerry Just Beat Every Estimate. The Market Still Doesn’t Know What It Actually Owns
Wall Street undervalues BlackBerry by pricing it as a legacy automotive royalty business instead of recognizing its role as a safety-certified operating system platform used across software-defined vehicles, defense systems, robotics, and maritime tracking
Driven by recent earnings beats, a deep portfolio of unmodeled patents, and new collaborations with companies like NVIDIA, the author projects massive financial upside if even half of these emerging verticals materialize by 2031
https://brian561249.substack.com/p/blackberry-just-beat-every-estimate

tweet.text:
A Guide to the US Financialization of Compute

We frequently receive questions about compute trading from neoclouds, GPU-as-a-service providers, data center operators, training and inference companies, energy companies, HFTs, brokerages, investment banks, FCMs, CTAs, RIAs, ETF issuers, VCs, and others. Here's a current summary of the market structure and participants:

Three main participants in the US compute derivatives universe:

1. CFTC-regulated derivatives exchanges (DCMs)

Definition
• Exchanges where CFTC-regulated futures, options on futures, and swaps can be legally traded in the US.
• Designs, certifies, and lists derivatives contracts. Engages with third-party index providers for cash-settled derivatives’ underlying settlement prices.
• Facilitates capital formation and investment in new US commodity, currency, and energy products.
• Responsible for market monitoring, position limits, circuit breakers, recordkeeping, and protection against market manipulation.

Participants

Why Token Optimization Is a Gift to the Hyperscalers
The shift from "token maxing" to "token optimization" (intelligently routing simpler tasks to cheaper, open-weight models) ultimately accelerates total token volume and drives high-margin infrastructure revenue straight to hyperscalers who act as the essential tollbooths of the AI ecosystem
https://www.uncoveralpha.com/p/why-token-optimization-is-a-gift
Dylan Patel of SemiAnalysis: Why Hardware-Software Co-Design Is AI's Real 100x

The AI Economy: The Next Chapter
The AI industry is transitioning from chasing the smartest frontier models to optimizing intelligence per dollar, as inference costs plummet and enterprises shift from pilots to production-scale economics, causing most routine workloads to favor efficient models over premium ones
Economic value will increasingly migrate to hyperscalers who own deployed compute infrastructure and orchestration layers (such as AWS Bedrock and Azure AI), which route tasks for cost, compliance, and performance, with geopolitical regulations further reinforcing their central role over pure model developers
